task_completion.description: Open the Floreant POS Back Office
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Floreant POS application is running and the 'Back Office' dashboard or main menu is visible.
A 'Configuration', 'Store Configuration', or similar settings window/dialog is open within the Back Office.

task_completion.description: Enter the service charge name 'Large Party'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text input field (likely for Service Charge, Gratuity, or Discount name) contains the exact text 'Large Party'.

task_completion.description: Enter the service charge rate of 18%
task_completion.visual_evidence: A numeric input field (likely for Rate, Percentage, or Amount) associated with the service charge contains the value '18' or '18.0'.

task_completion.description: Save the new service charge configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks 'Save', 'Apply', or 'OK' in the configuration window, or the new 'Large Party' service charge is visible in a saved list of configurations.

integrity.description: Agent used the Floreant POS GUI instead of terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Floreant POS graphical user interface. The agent should not be using a terminal to execute SQL commands or edit database files directly.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Floreant POS application
integrity.visual_evidence: The window being interacted with is clearly the Java-based Floreant POS application, not a text editor, web browser, or mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply type the expected answer into an unrelated text file
integrity.visual_evidence: The values 'Large Party' and '18' are entered into the correct functional input fields within the POS configuration, not just written into a blank document or terminal echo command.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Floreant POS uses an Apache Derby database located at /opt/floreantpos/database/derby-server/posdb. In this version (1.4 build 1707b), service charges are typically handled as store-level configuration values in the GUI rather than separate named database entities in a dedicated SERVICE_CHARGE table. The agent must use the Floreant POS Back Office GUI to configure the service charge, specifically looking for store configuration, gratuity, or discount settings to input the name 'Large Party' and the rate '18'.
